'''Albert Kelly''' (born 21 March 1991) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fe|}} or {{rlp|hb}} for the [[Cessnock Goannas]] in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]].

He has previously played for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL), and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also spent time with the [[Souths Logan Magpies]] in the Hostplus Cup.

==Early career==
Kelly was born in [[Macksville, New South Wales]], [[Australia]] and grew up in [[Kempsey, New South Wales]]. He is an [[Aboriginal Australians|Australian Aborigine]] of the [[Gumbaynggirr]] people.

He started playing at the age of five, he is a product of [[Group 2 Rugby League|Group 2]] junior rugby league.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Nick |first1=Houghton |title=Sharks star could play in Group 2 |url=https://www.coffscoastadvocate.com.au/news/sharks-star-could-play-group-2/1305668/ |access-date=15 March 2012 |work=The Coffs Coast Advocate |agency=The Coffs Coast Advocate |publisher=Nick Houghton}}</ref> He attended [[St Paul's College, Kempsey]]<ref>{{cite web |title=Another Junior doing well |url=https://forums.leagueunlimited.com/threads/another-junior-doing-well.146914/ |website=league unlimited |date=10 July 2006 |publisher=Jake the snake |access-date=10 July 2006}}</ref> before moving to Sydney and attending [[Patrician Brothers' College, Blacktown]]. He was selected in the New South Wales under-15s Combined Catholic High Schools' rugby league side as five-eighth. In 2008, he played for the Australian Schoolboys where he was vice-captain. He was chased by the [[Sydney Roosters]] and [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], before being signed by the [[Parramatta Eels]] on a four-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=TOYOTA CUP SQUAD NAMED FOR SEASON 2009 |url=https://www.ourfootyteam.com/toyotaCup.php |website=ourfootyteam |publisher=Parramatta Eels |access-date=17 November 2008}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
[[File:Albert Kelly Parramatta.jpg|thumbnail|right|Kelly playing for the Eels]]
In 2008 to 2010, Kelly played [[NRL Under-20s|Toyota Cup]] for the [[Parramatta Eels]]' Toyota Cup team, scoring 11 tries in 23 games, and also played for the [[New South Wales Under-18's rugby league team|New South Wales under-18s]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/k/kelly_albert/index.php |title=Monday Morning Halfback: Week 3 Review |website=www.rleague.com |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110909234124/http://www.rleague.com//db/player/k/kelly_albert/index.php |archive-date=9 September 2011}}</ref>

He then played for Cronulla's Toyota Cup team in 2010, scoring three tries in 11 games.

===Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks===
[[File:Albert Kelly.jpg|thumbnail|right|Kelly Playing for the Sharks in 2010]]
In Round 1 of the [[2010 NRL season]] he made his [[NRL]] début for Cronulla-Sutherland against the [[Melbourne Storm]], but played only five matches that year due to injury. He returned in Round 1 of the 2011 season. He scored his first two [[NRL]] tries against the [[Penrith Panthers]] in Round 3 of the [[2011 NRL season]]. In Round 7, he broke his leg after an attempted tackle playing against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]]. Kelly returned for the last two matches of the season. In 2012, Kelly fell out of favour with Cronulla coach [[Shane Flanagan]] and was sacked mid-season.

===2012 Newcastle Rugby League===
[[File:albertkellyknights.jpg|thumbnail|right|Kelly playing for Newcastle in 2012]]
In 2012, he signed with the Charlestown-based Central Newcastle Butcher Boys in the [[Newcastle Rugby League]]. While signed to Central, Kelly also trained with the [[Newcastle Knights]] from early April, as the Knights' coach, [[Wayne Bennett (rugby league)|Wayne Bennett]], offered Kelly help in getting his career and life back on track.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/sacked-sharks-bad-boy-albert-kelly-thrown-a-lifeline-by-the-newcastle-knights/story-e6frexnr-1226320111224|title=Sacked Sharks Bad Boy Albert Kelly Thrown a Lifeline|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=10 August 2015}}</ref> He then signed with the Knights to play in the [[NSW Cup|New South Wales Cup]] competition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/NSW-Cup-team/53503 |title=NSW Cup team - Newcastle Knights |access-date=28 June 2012 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20121231052937/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news-display/NSW-Cup-team/53503 |archive-date=31 December 2012 |url-status=dead |df=dmy-all }}</ref>

In June 2012, Kelly was suspended before having his contract terminated after pleading guilty in court to smashing a light at Newcastle nightclub Fannys.<ref>{{cite web|last=Leeson|first=Joshua|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/205081/albert-kelly-sacked-by-knights/|title=Albert Kelly sacked by Knights | Newcastle Herald|publisher=Theherald.com.au|date=28 June 2012|access-date=10 August 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/albert-kelly-sacked-by-newcastle-knights/story-e6frexnr-1226411190682|title=Albert Kelly sacked by Newcastle Knights|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=10 August 2015}}</ref>

===Gold Coast Titans===
In 2013, Kelly joined the [[Gold Coast Titans]] to trial for a contract. He made his début for the Titans against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]]. They lost the game 12–10, however there were good signs for Kelly, producing a try assist. In his next game, he scored a double and another try assist against the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the 36-0 demolition. On 22 August 2014, Kelly became one of the current NRL players and former Sharks players to accept reduced bans from the [[Australian Sports Anti-Doping Authority]] for his role in the club's 2011 [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks supplements saga|supplements program]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/cronulla-sharks-players-accept-doping-bans-20140822-10728y.html|title=Cronulla Sharks players accept doping bans|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=22 August 2014|access-date=29 August 2014}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
On 18 October 2014, Kelly was released from the Titans alongside [[Maurice Blair]] to sign two-year contracts with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], replacing Australian halves [[Travis Burns (rugby league)|Travis Burns]] and [[Kris Keating]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Michael Carayannis|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/gold-coast-titans/gold-coast-titans-release-albert-kelly-and-maurice-blair-20141018-118547.html|title=Gold Coast Titans release Albert Kelly and Maurice Blair|publisher=Sydney Morning Herald|date=18 October 2014|access-date=10 August 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/titans-release-blair-and-kelly/tabid/10874/newsid/82584/default.aspx?cid=NRL_HP_Latest|title=Titans release Blair and Kelly|publisher=NRL.com|date=18 October 2014|access-date=10 August 2015}}</ref> On 30 September 2015, he was named Hull KR Player of the Year.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Albert-Kelly-big-winner-Hull-KR-awards-dinner/story-27890145-detail/story.html|title=Albert Kelly the big winner at Hull KR awards dinner|newspaper=Hull Daily Mail|date=30 September 2015|access-date=2 November 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151001182225/http://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/Albert-Kelly-big-winner-Hull-KR-awards-dinner/story-27890145-detail/story.html|archive-date=1 October 2015|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref> In 2015, Kelly played in the [[2015 Challenge Cup]] final suffering a loss of 50–0 against [[Leeds Rhinos]].

===Hull F.C.===
After two seasons at Hull Kingston Rovers he moved to rivals Hull F.C.<ref>The Guardian , June 2017 salford hull super league match [https://r.search.yahoo.com/_ylt=A0LEVx1VlnxZU78A509XNyoA;_ylu=X3oDMTEyN3VycGp1BGNvbG8DYmYxBHBvcwM1BHZ0aWQDVUkyQzNfMQRzZWMDc3I-/RV=2/RE=1501365974/RO=10/RU=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/jun/09/salford-hull-super-league-match-report/RK=1/RS=4rDjaQJd1O5oxkeM_FZ0HOryoWA- salford hull super league match]{{Dead link|date=September 2019 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> On 26 August 2017, Kelly played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup]] final for Hull F.C., winning the game 18–14 against the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|work=Daily Mirror|date=26 August 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}</ref>

He was also named in the [[Super League Dream Team|2017 Super League Dream Team]] and finishing as Man of Steel runner up 2017.

In 2018 footage emerged of Kelly verbally abusing a female [[McDonald's]] employee while intoxicated. Hull FC released a statement a few days later saying the incident had been dealt with internally.<ref name="Hull FC rule on Albert Kelly's drunken tirade">{{cite web |title=Hull FC rule on Albert Kelly's drunken tirade |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-rule-albert-kellys-drunken-tirade-299941 |website=Yorkshire Post |date=23 April 2018|access-date=26 May 2020}}</ref>

===Brisbane Broncos===
Kelly joined Brisbane on a train and trial contract ahead of the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>[https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-albert-kelly-brisbane-broncos-preseason-training-kevin-walters/news-story/ecf71ee8217fa616faa9ad3a0074e0a3 ‘He’s piled on a few kilos’: New Broncos recruit sent back to feeder club]</ref> He spent the early part of 2021 playing for the [[Souths Logan Magpies]] in the [[Queensland Cup|Hostplus Cup]]. While playing for the Magpies in March 2021, Kelly suffered a laceration to his right ear after clashing heads with another player.<ref>{{cite web|last=Badel|first=Peter|date=23 May 2021|title=NRL 2021: Gruesome reason behind Broncos hero Albert Kelly's ear guard
|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2021-gruesome-reason-behind-broncos-hero-albert-kellys-ear-guard/news-story/60b034330f1608102c77aa8c6d406058|work=Courier Mail|access-date=23 May 2021}}</ref>

He was selected in the halves to replace the dropped [[Anthony Milford]] ahead of the round 11 fixture against the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>[https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tips/nrl-2021-team-tips-supercoach-raiders-v-storm-papenhuyzen-wighton-papalii-dragons-v-sharks-josh-mcguire-karmichael-hunt-brisbane-broncos/news-story/8108dca8957660d27a786a0d028b40d0?recommendedCount=0 Bellamy’s radical No.6 selection as Kevvie changes up his halves AGAIN: Teams Analysis]</ref><ref>[https://www.broncos.com.au/news/2021/05/18/team-list-round-11-two-broncos-debutants-among-four-changes/ Team List Round 11: Two Broncos Debutants Among Four Changes]</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=St John|first=Mark|date=22 May 2021|title=Broncos answer Kevvie's 'embarrassing' call, Walker's 'first real test': 3 Big Hits|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-sydney-roosters-vs-brisbane-broncos-round-11-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-sam-walker-payne-haas/news-story/7ca81517ad9318ee14b4aedd903e99ae/|work=Fox Sports|access-date=22 May 2021}}</ref>

On 3 April 2022, it was announced that Kelly had been placed under investigation by the Brisbane club after video footage emerged which showed Kelly being involved in a drunken fight with teammate [[Payne Haas]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/broncos-stars-payne-haas-and-albert-kelly-in-alleged-drunken-fight/news-story/bf78002de8188b3cbe831fd6102ce86e/|title=Brisbane stars Payne Haas and Albert Kelly in alleged drunken fight|website=www.news.com au}}</ref>

===Redcliffe Dolphins===
On 18 January 2023, Kelly signed a contract to join Redcliffe in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-hull-fc-and-hull-kr-star-albert-kelly-finds-new-club-following-brisbane-broncos-release/|title=Ex-Hull FC and Hull KR star Albert Kelly finds new club following Brisbane Broncos release|website=www.totalrl.com|date=18 January 2023 }}</ref>

===Souths Magpies===
In November 2023, Kelly signed a contract to join Souths Magpies in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url= https://www.zerotackle.com/ex-bronco-re-signs-with-australian-club-165424/ |title=Ex-Bronco re-signs with Australian club|website=www.zerotackle.com|date=19 May 2024 }}</ref>

==Honours==
* Challenge Cup: (1) 2017
* Super League Dream Team: (1) 2017

==Personal life==
Kelly is a cousin of player [[Greg Inglis]].<ref name="Albert Kelly">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/54262/Default.aspx|title=Albert Kelly|publisher=NRL|date=30 March 2009|access-date=15 March 2010|archive-date=27 September 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120927180616/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/54262/Default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref>
